nodes.name: 01_README.md
nodes.summary: Course note introducing the three building blocks of a web page — HTML for structure, CSS for design, and JS for behavior — written in Korean as the opening lesson of the HTML module.

nodes.name: 02_README.md
nodes.summary: Development-environment setup note walking through creating a working folder and opening it in VSCode, including the workspace-trust prompt.

nodes.name: 03_index.html
nodes.summary: First hands-on HTML page demonstrating the basic document skeleton (doctype, html, head, body) with headings, divs, and a canvas element, annotated with Korean beginner comments.
nodes.languageNotes: Every tag carries an inline Korean comment explaining its role, a teaching pattern repeated across all exercises in this module.

nodes.name: 04_index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise teaching HTML comments — how to leave invisible notes and temporarily disable markup — using escaped comment syntax shown as visible page text.

nodes.name: 05_index.html
nodes.summary: Minimal HTML page showing the smallest complete document: doctype, head with charset and title, and a body containing a single h1 heading.

nodes.name: 06_index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise demonstrating the six heading levels (h1 through h6) and the paragraph tag, illustrating how heading size decreases with level.

nodes.name: 07_index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise on tag nesting and inline emphasis, using strong inside a paragraph plus HTML entities (<, >, &) to display reserved characters.

nodes.name: 08_index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise embedding an image with the img tag, covering the src, width, and alt attributes and the self-closing void-element form.

nodes.name: 09_index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise creating hyperlinks with the anchor tag, linking to Google, Naver, and Claude to show the href attribute and full URL requirement.

nodes.name: 10_index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise combining semantic layout tags (header, main, footer) with an unordered list, framing each section as a labeled box with a meaningful name.

nodes.name: 11_README.md
nodes.summary: Assignment sheet directing the student to build a self-introduction site in 11_index.html using all tags learned so far, plus homework covering GitHub repo creation, blog posting, and Google Drive uploads.

nodes.name: 11_index.html
nodes.summary: Capstone self-introduction page for the HTML module, combining semantic layout (header, main, footer), an image, nested ordered and unordered lists of hobbies and books, strong emphasis, and an external blog link.
nodes.languageNotes: Nests ol elements alongside li items inside a ul — a common beginner pattern that works visually but places the ordered lists as siblings of, rather than children of, their list items.

nodes.name: 12_index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise on ordered lists, using an ol with three morning-routine items to show automatic numbering when sequence matters.

nodes.name: 13_index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise on line breaks and horizontal rules, demonstrating the br and hr void elements inside and between paragraphs.

nodes.name: 14_index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise building a simple class timetable with the table element, introducing tr rows, th header cells, td data cells, and the border attribute.

nodes.name: 15_index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise creating an input form with labeled text and number fields plus a submit button, introducing form, label, input, and button elements.

nodes.name: 16_index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise embedding two YouTube videos via iframe elements, covering the src embed URL, width/height sizing, and permission attributes.

nodes.name: 17_index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise embedding a Google Maps view of a location using a single iframe with an embed URL, lazy loading, and referrer policy attributes.

nodes.name: 18_index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise introducing the class attribute as a reusable label, applying the same class name to two paragraphs to show it may repeat across elements.

nodes.name: 19_index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise introducing the id attribute as a page-unique label, contrasting it with class by tagging a heading and a paragraph with distinct ids.

nodes.name: 20_index.html
nodes.summary: HTML exercise demonstrating the div element as a block-level container, grouping an image, name, and price into a single product-card unit.
nodes.languageNotes: Every line carries a Korean beginner-oriented comment explaining what the tag does.

nodes.name: 21_index.html
nodes.summary: HTML exercise demonstrating the span element as an inline container, highlighting part of a sentence with inline styles without breaking the line.

nodes.name: 22_index.html
nodes.summary: HTML exercise on image source paths, contrasting a relative path to a parent-folder image with absolute https URLs.

nodes.name: 23_index.html
nodes.summary: HTML exercise on advanced anchor usage: opening links in a new tab with target=_blank, in-page fragment jumps (#top), and mailto:/tel: protocol links.

nodes.name: 24_index.html
nodes.summary: HTML exercise touring form input types: email, password, checkbox, radio, date, textarea, select dropdown, and a submit control.
nodes.languageNotes: The markup is intentionally rough student work: a label element is opened but closed with a stray </form> tag, and there is no wrapping form element.

nodes.name: 25_index.html
nodes.summary: HTML exercise showcasing inline text-formatting tags: em, mark, small, code, blockquote, and sub/sup for subscripts and superscripts.

nodes.name: 26_index.html
nodes.summary: HTML exercise on merging table cells with colspan and rowspan inside a thead/tbody grade-report table.

nodes.name: 27_index.html
nodes.summary: HTML exercise using details/summary to build a native collapsible 'show answer' box without JavaScript.

nodes.name: 28_README.md
nodes.summary: Homework note for lesson 28 asking students to push the day's practice code to GitHub and write a blog post about a chosen topic.

nodes.name: 28_index.html
nodes.summary: HTML exercise laying out a full semantic page skeleton with header, nav, main, section, article, aside, and footer landmarks.

nodes.name: 29_index.html
nodes.summary: HTML exercise embedding a video with the video element, combining autoplay, muted, controls, and width attributes with a relative src path.

nodes.name: 30_index.html
nodes.summary: HTML exercise embedding audio with multiple source fallbacks (mp3 then ogg) plus fallback text for unsupported browsers.

nodes.name: 31_index.html
nodes.summary: Bare practice page with an unordered list, class and id labelled divs, a heading, and a link — a base document for experimenting with selectors and dev tools.

nodes.name: 32_README.md
nodes.summary: Study note on browser developer tools (F12): how to open them, inspect elements, live-edit styles for preview, and read Console errors, with tips for learning from other sites.

nodes.name: 33_README.md
nodes.summary: Setup note listing steps and links for installing Claude Code on Windows via winget and running it from Windows Terminal.

nodes.name: 33_index.html
nodes.summary: Quiz-style review page: a semantic sign-up page (header/nav, form with label-for/id pairing, fieldset, table) annotated with 11 numbered comprehension questions in comments.
nodes.languageNotes: Each construct is paired with a Korean comment posing a 'what happens if' question — the file doubles as a self-test worksheet.

nodes.name: 34_index.html
nodes.summary: Mini-project: a complete personal portfolio page (intro, career, skills, works, Q&A, contact) built with HTML only — no CSS or JavaScript — using tables, font/bgcolor attributes, progress bars, image maps, datalist, and details/summary.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately styles everything with legacy presentational HTML (font, bgcolor, align, table layout, center) since CSS is off-limits, while still mixing in modern elements like picture, progress, datalist, output, and abbr.

nodes.name: .stylelintrc.json
nodes.summary: Stylelint configuration extending the standard config and enabling the stylelint-order plugin to enforce alphabetical CSS property ordering in this exercise's stylesheets.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the extends/plugins/rules pattern: a shared base config (stylelint-config-standard) is layered with a plugin rule (order/properties-alphabetical-order) for automatic property sorting.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Minimal Korean-language demo page that links styles/main.css and renders a single .box element to visually verify the Stylelint property-ordering exercise.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Package manifest declaring only devDependencies for the linting exercise: htmlhint, stylelint, stylelint-config-standard, and stylelint-order.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Front-end page for JS exercise 17 (server-connected CRUD): renders four buttons (CREATE, READ, UPDATE, DELETE) and an output area, wired to fetch-based REST calls in scripts/main.js against a local json-server backend.
nodes.languageNotes: Korean-language (lang="ko") page; a note instructs the learner to run npm install and npm start to launch the json-server backend before using the buttons.

nodes.name: db.json
nodes.summary: json-server database file holding the live users collection (four user records) that backs the REST endpoints exercised by the CRUD demo; mutated in place as the exercise's CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE calls run.
nodes.languageNotes: The inconsistent key ordering and duplicate 'John' entries show the file was mutated by json-server POST/PUT calls rather than authored by hand.

nodes.name: db-1783407269920.json
nodes.summary: Timestamped snapshot of the json-server users database (two seed users, Kim and Lee), created as an automatic backup of db.json before the CRUD exercise mutated it.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Stock Vite + React template README explaining the scaffold's HMR setup via @vitejs/plugin-react, the optional React Compiler, and pointers for expanding the ESLint configuration.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite entry HTML page for the React app; provides the #root mount element and loads src/main.jsx as an ES module to bootstrap React rendering.
nodes.languageNotes: In Vite projects, index.html is the true build entry point — the bundler follows the <script type="module"> tag to discover the JavaScript graph.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: NPM manifest for the my-app Vite + React 19 starter, defining dev/build/lint/preview scripts, React 19 runtime dependencies, and Vite 8 plus ESLint tooling as devDependencies.
nodes.languageNotes: "type": "module" makes all .js files in the package ES modules by default, which is why the app's config files use ESM syntax.

nodes.name: 01_README.md
nodes.summary: One-line note pointing to the Visual Studio Code download page, marking the starting point of the VS Code setup lesson.

nodes.name: 02_README.md
nodes.summary: Bullet list (in Korean) of recommended VS Code extensions for the course, covering Live Server, Prettier, ESLint, Auto Rename Tag, GitLens, and Error Lens with a short purpose note for each.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: First CSS lesson page demonstrating basic selectors: heavily annotated HTML with an h1, plain and class-tagged paragraphs, and a list whose em element is styled via a descendant selector in the linked stylesheet.
nodes.languageNotes: Every line carries a Korean teaching comment explaining what the tag does and which CSS rule targets it.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Stylesheet for lesson 01 contrasting the three fundamental selector kinds: element (h1, p), class (.special, .normal), and descendant (li em), each setting a distinct text color.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Lesson page for CSS rule-ordering conventions, reusing the heading/paragraph/list structure so the stylesheet can show rules ordered from general to specific.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Stylesheet demonstrating the general-to-specific ordering convention: body defaults first, then element selectors, then a class selector, and finally a descendant selector, with numbered Korean comments labeling each tier.

nodes.name: .htmlhintrc
nodes.summary: Minimal HTMLHint configuration enabling three lint rules — tag-pair, id-unique, and alt-require — for the lesson 03 HTML page.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Single .box rule with its properties listed alphabetically, used in the lesson about property ordering and CSS linting (paired with the folder's HTMLHint/Stylelint tooling exercises).

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Box-model lesson page containing two ul boxes that the stylesheet decorates with padding, border, and margin to visualize the CSS box model.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Stylesheet applying padding, border, and margin to ul elements to demonstrate each layer of the CSS box model, with list bullets removed.

nodes.name: .stylelintrc.json
nodes.summary: Stylelint configuration extending stylelint-config-standard and enabling the stylelint-order plugin to enforce alphabetical CSS property ordering in lesson 05.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Flexbox introduction page with a three-item ul.menu navigation list that the stylesheet lays out horizontally via display:flex; note the file contains an accidentally duplicated head preamble.
nodes.languageNotes: The first five lines (comment, doctype, html, head, meta) are duplicated — a copy-paste artifact that browsers tolerate but linters would flag.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Stylesheet introducing flexbox: .menu uses display:flex with gap to line list items up horizontally, and li rules style each item as a rounded light-blue pill.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Lesson page demonstrating the three ways to attach CSS: an external stylesheet link, an internal style block coloring paragraphs green, and an inline style attribute coloring one paragraph red.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: One-rule external stylesheet (h1 navy) serving as the 'external' example in the lesson comparing external, internal, and inline CSS.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Pseudo-class lesson page containing a single external link used to demonstrate the four anchor states (:link, :visited, :hover, :active) styled in the linked stylesheet.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Stylesheet assigning a distinct color to each of the four anchor pseudo-classes (a:link, a:visited, a:hover, a:active) in the conventional LVHA order.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: 3D card-flip lesson page with two .card containers, each holding an .inner wrapper with stacked .front and .back faces that the stylesheet rotates on hover.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Implements the classic 3D card-flip effect: perspective on .card, preserve-3d and a transform transition on .inner, hover-triggered rotateY(180deg), and backface-visibility:hidden on the absolutely positioned front/back faces.
nodes.languageNotes: Shows the full 3D-flip recipe — perspective, transform-style: preserve-3d, backface-visibility, and a pre-rotated back face — each annotated with a Korean explanation.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Hover-transform lesson page containing a single .box div that spins and scales up on mouse-over via the linked stylesheet.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Styles a flex-centered rounded .box and adds a :hover rule combining rotate(360deg) and scale(1.5), eased over 0.9 seconds via a transform transition.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Keyframe-animation lesson page with two ball divs (.ball1, .ball2) that the stylesheet animates automatically — one floating vertically, one sliding horizontally.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Defines two @keyframes animations — float (translateY bounce) and slide (translateX shuttle) — and applies each to a circular ball element on an infinite 1.5-second ease-in-out loop.
nodes.languageNotes: Introduces @keyframes as a named 'script' of motion that the animation shorthand then plays, a mental model spelled out in the Korean comments.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise page for CSS exercise 11 (neon gradient card): renders a single .neon box that the sibling stylesheet turns into a glowing gradient card.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Styles the neon card exercise: a flex-centered box with a 135-degree linear-gradient background, a pink box-shadow glow, and a hover transition that spins and scales the card.
nodes.languageNotes: Demonstrates the flex centering trio (display: flex + align-items + justify-content) combined with transform on :hover for animated feedback.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise page for CSS positioning: a relative parent containing relative and absolute children, plus fixed and sticky elements to observe scroll behavior.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Demonstrates all five CSS position values: a relative parent anchoring an absolute child, a relative offset box, a viewport-fixed badge with z-index, and a sticky bar that pins to the top on scroll.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an artificially tall body (min-height: 1500px) to force scrolling so fixed vs. sticky behavior can be compared.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise page for CSS Grid: six card divs inside a .grid container that the stylesheet lays out as a three-column grid.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Minimal CSS Grid demo: display: grid with repeat(3, 1fr) columns and gap, auto-flowing six cards into two rows of three.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise page for responsive design: five cards in a flex .box container whose direction changes with a media query in the sibling stylesheet.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Media-query demo: a row of flex: 1 gradient cards that switches to a vertical column layout below a 600px viewport width via @media (max-width: 600px).

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise page for CSS custom properties: two buttons that share the same variable-driven color defined in the sibling stylesheet.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: CSS variables demo: defines --main-color, --gap, and --body-color on :root and consumes them with var() so a single edit recolors both buttons and the page background.
nodes.languageNotes: Shows the :root definition plus var() consumption pattern, the foundation for single-source theming.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise page for visual effects: a single hoverable card demonstrating box-shadow, border-radius, opacity, and transition together.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Combines four effect properties on one card — border-radius, opacity, box-shadow, and transition: all — with a :hover state that lifts the card via translateY, sharpens opacity, and morphs it toward a circle.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise page for pseudo-elements: three paragraphs decorated by ::before/::after rules — a star prefix, wrapping quotation marks, and an underline bar.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Pseudo-element practice covering three patterns: ::before content injection (star prefix), paired ::before/::after quotation marks, and an empty-content ::after block styled as a decorative underline.
nodes.languageNotes: Highlights that a pseudo-element never renders without a content property, including the empty-string content: "" trick for drawing boxes.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise page for the universal CSS reset: a heading and paragraph used to compare default browser margins against the reset applied in the sibling stylesheet.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Three-line universal reset: * { margin: 0; padding: 0; box-sizing: border-box; } — the standard baseline that removes default browser spacing and unifies size calculation.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Capstone mini-project page in an 8-bit retro arcade style: a full 'OS window' layout containing a hero with confetti, a design-concept grid, a CSS Design Awards site review card, nine HTML/CSS concept cards, and a five-question multiple-choice quiz with a score counter and hidden ending screen — all driven purely by HTML and CSS.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses radio inputs with paired labels as the quiz mechanism (no JavaScript), plus aria-hidden decorative layers and an sr-only heading for accessibility. Pulls the Galmuri pixel font from a CDN.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: 1,148-line capstone stylesheet implementing the retro pixel theme end to end: a :root two-color neon palette with pixel fonts, clip-path stair-step corners, CRT scanline and border-glow keyframe animations, a marquee ticker, and a JavaScript-free quiz engine built from :has()/:checked selectors with a CSS counter scoreboard that unlocks an all-clear ending screen.
nodes.languageNotes: Notably implements interactive scoring in pure CSS: .quiz-card:has(.quiz-radio--correct:checked) increments counter(quiz-score) rendered via ::after, and an article:has() chain of all five correct answers swaps the ending panels. Also includes @media breakpoints and a prefers-reduced-motion fallback.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise page for JS event handling: a heading and a button wired up by the sibling script, with the stylesheet linked in head and the script loaded just before body close.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Event-listener exercise: selects the title and button via querySelector, then iterates an event-name-to-message map with Object.entries to register click, dblclick, keydown, and keyup listeners that swap the heading text.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Object.entries + destructuring in a forEach to register multiple event listeners from a data object instead of repeating addEventListener calls.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Minimal styling for the event exercise: tomato-colored large title text and a padded button with a pointer cursor.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise page for reading input values: a name input, a greet button, and an output paragraph updated by the sibling script.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Input-value exercise: on button click, reads the text field's .value and writes a personalized greeting into the output paragraph using a template literal.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Minimal styling for the greeting exercise: bold enlarged output text and padded input and button controls.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise page for JavaScript data types: seven buttons (string, number, boolean, undefined, null, array, object) and an output paragraph that the sibling script fills with each value and its type.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: JavaScript data-types exercise: an arrow function `show` prints a clicked value with its `typeof` result, wired to seven buttons covering string, number, boolean, undefined, null, array, and object.
nodes.languageNotes: Demonstrates the classic `typeof null === "object"` quirk and uses JSON.stringify to render objects and arrays.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Minimal styling for the data-types exercise: button padding/spacing and a bold, larger output line (#out).

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Markup for a mini calculator exercise: two number inputs, an operator select (+, -, *, /), a compute button, and an output paragraph, linked to its sibling stylesheet and script.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Conditional-logic exercise implementing a four-operation calculator: converts input strings with Number(), branches on the selected operator with if/else if, and renders the equation via a template literal.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Styling for the calculator exercise: narrow number inputs, padded select/button controls, and a bold result line.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Markup for an if/else-if exercise: an ice-cream flavor select, a confirm button, and a result paragraph, linked to its sibling stylesheet and script.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Branching exercise: on button click, compares the selected flavor with strict equality and writes a different message for chocolate, vanilla, or any other choice using if / else if / else.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Styling for the flavor-check exercise: bold enlarged result text and padded select/button controls.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Markup for an arrow-function exercise: two number inputs separated by a multiplication sign, a multiply button, and an output paragraph, linked to its sibling stylesheet and script.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Function-basics exercise: defines a one-line arrow function `multiply(num1, num2)` with implicit return and calls it from a click handler to display the product via a template literal.
nodes.languageNotes: Teaches implicit return in single-expression arrow functions (no braces or `return` keyword needed).

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Styling for the multiply exercise: bold enlarged result text, narrow number inputs, and padded button.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Markup for a countdown-click exercise: a button and a span showing the remaining count starting at 100, linked to its sibling stylesheet and script.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: State-mutation exercise: keeps a mutable `let` counter starting at 100 and decrements it on each button click, updating the span with a template literal.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Styling for the countdown exercise: a large pointer-cursor button and bold enlarged count display.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Markup for an image-toggle exercise: an img element with alt text and a hint paragraph, linked to its sibling stylesheet and script.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Attribute-manipulation exercise: toggles an image between two picsum.photos URLs on click by reading the current src with getAttribute and swapping it with setAttribute.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the element `onclick` property instead of addEventListener, and requires network access for the placeholder image service.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Styling for the image-toggle exercise: rounded image corners with pointer cursor and a gray hint line.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Markup for a localStorage exercise: a greeting heading, name input, and save/clear buttons, linked to its sibling stylesheet and script.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Browser-persistence exercise: greets a saved name on load via localStorage.getItem, stores the entered name with setItem on save, and deletes it with removeItem on reset.
nodes.languageNotes: Covers the full localStorage API triad (getItem, setItem, removeItem) with an empty-input guard clause.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Styling for the greeting exercise: purple greeting heading plus padded, spaced inputs and buttons.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Markup for a loops exercise: a number input, repeat and countdown buttons, and an empty ul list to fill, linked to its sibling stylesheet and script.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Loops exercise with two handlers: a for loop appending list items counting up from 1 to N, and a while loop counting down from N to 1, both building li elements with createElement/appendChild.
nodes.languageNotes: Contrasts for and while loops side by side, including an inline warning about infinite loops when the counter is not decremented.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Styling for the loops exercise: padded spaced buttons and an enlarged output list with generous line height.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Markup for a string-methods exercise: a text input prefilled with "hello", an analyze button, and an output paragraph, linked to its sibling stylesheet and script.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: String-methods exercise: on click, reports the input text's length and the results of toLowerCase, toUpperCase, and replaceAll("e", "E"), assembled with template literals into innerHTML.
nodes.languageNotes: Distinguishes the `.length` property from string methods, and uses replaceAll (ES2021) rather than replace.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Minimal stylesheet for the exercise-12 DOM input demo, adding padding to inputs and buttons and enlarging the #out result text.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise page for JavaScript array basics: a fruit-name input, an add button, and output paragraphs wired to the sibling script and stylesheet.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Array-basics exercise: keeps a fruits array, renders it with join() plus a length count, and pushes new items from the input on button click.
nodes.languageNotes: Demonstrates Array.push/join, template literals, and arrow-function event handlers with Korean teaching comments.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Styles for the fruit-list exercise: input/button padding, bold fruit list, and gray count caption.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise page for JavaScript object basics with an output paragraph and two buttons (age +1, rename) driven by the sibling script.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Object-basics exercise: defines a person object, renders its properties, increments age on click, and toggles the name with a ternary expression.
nodes.languageNotes: Teaches dot-notation property access, mutation, and the ternary operator for toggling values.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Styles for the object exercise: large purple output text and spaced buttons.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Exercise page for JavaScript class basics with a single 'make them bark' button and an output paragraph.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Class-basics exercise: defines a Dog class with constructor and bark method, instantiates two dogs, and prints both barks on button click.
nodes.languageNotes: Introduces ES class syntax, constructor, this-binding, and instantiation with new.

nodes.name: Dog
nodes.summary: Teaching class that stores a dog's name in its constructor and returns a name-prefixed bark string from its bark() method.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Styles for the class exercise: bold output line and padded button.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Bare exercise page for the debugging lesson: a single buy button plus the sibling script; intentionally has no stylesheet.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Debugging exercise demonstrating console.log, typeof checks, the debugger statement, and a commented walkthrough of the common null-querySelector TypeError.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the debugger statement to pause execution in DevTools and explains the 'Cannot read properties of null' error pattern.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Fetch-based CRUD client exercise against a local json-server users API: four buttons issue POST, GET, PUT, and DELETE requests and print normalized results to the page.
nodes.languageNotes: Demonstrates async/await with fetch, JSON request headers/bodies, and normalizing response key order before display.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Styles for the CRUD exercise: flex button row and a dark console-like #out result panel with pre-wrap text.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: App shell for the MORNING SLOT capstone: animated sky scene, three slot-machine reels for clothes/food/music, jackpot rate badge and guide modal, result summary, and archive views.

nodes.name: planning.md
nodes.summary: Korean-language project plan for the MORNING SLOT website covering concept, target users, screen layout, slot-machine and jackpot mechanics, weather/location features, record storage, animations, responsive strategy, and tech stack.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Complete logic for the MORNING SLOT capstone: 3D cylinder reel spins with easing, jackpot combo detection with confetti/Web Audio celebration, Open-Meteo weather with geolocation fallbacks, and a localStorage-backed archive and daily streak.
nodes.languageNotes: Notable patterns: requestAnimationFrame easing for 3D CSS reels, lazy AudioContext creation, canvas particle confetti, and three successive fetchCurrentLocation declarations where the last (fixed-location) definition shadows the earlier geolocation versions.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Extensive stylesheet for MORNING SLOT: gold/sky design tokens, animated sun-cloud-bird background, 3D perspective slot reels, jackpot guide modal, result cards, archive lists, and responsive breakpoints.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses CSS custom properties for theming and 3D transforms with perspective for the rotating reel drums.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Page for the Shared Dot Garden capstone: hero with test/formal mode switch, stats dashboard with progress bar, color palette, main garden canvas, minimap, and timelapse controls.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Canvas-based collaborative pixel garden: a 140x100 grid where visitors plant colored pixels with cooldown modes, persisted to localStorage, synced across tabs via BroadcastChannel, with age-based color fading, minimap, and timelapse replay of planting history.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses BroadcastChannel plus the storage event for cross-tab sync and requestAnimationFrame-driven planting animations.

nodes.name: main.css
nodes.summary: Stylesheet for the Shared Dot Garden: garden-themed green palette via CSS variables, stat cards, canvas panels, tooltip, palette swatches, and responsive layout.

nodes.name: eslint.config.js
nodes.summary: ESLint flat config for the Vite React app: applies the recommended JS rules plus react-hooks and react-refresh presets to all .js/.jsx files and ignores dist.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ESLint's flat config API (defineConfig, globalIgnores) rather than legacy .eslintrc.

nodes.name: App.css
nodes.summary: Component styles for the Vite React starter App: counter button, layered hero logo composition, tick dividers, and documentation/social card sections.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses native CSS nesting for hover/focus states inside selectors.

nodes.name: App.jsx
nodes.summary: Default Vite React 19 starter component: renders a hero with layered Vite/React logos, a useState click counter, and documentation/community link sections.
nodes.languageNotes: Function component using the useState hook and an SVG sprite (icons.svg) referenced via <use href>.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Root React component (default export) holding a count state and rendering the starter page's hero, counter button, and link cards.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet for the React starter: CSS custom properties for colors and fonts, light/dark color-scheme support, base typography, and responsive font sizing.

nodes.name: loadCustomOptions
nodes.summary: Reads a comma-separated custom option list from localStorage for a reel category, returning the defaults when nothing valid is saved.

nodes.name: renderJackpotGuide
nodes.summary: Renders the jackpot combo cards (title, three option labels, comment, accent color) into the guide modal via a template-literal map.

nodes.name: setupJackpotInfoRow
nodes.summary: Restructures the DOM so the jackpot-rate badge and guide button share a single info row, removing the original guide row.

nodes.name: getTodayWeather
nodes.summary: Produces deterministic pseudo-random morning/afternoon/evening weather slots seeded from today's date, used as an offline fallback.

nodes.name: getWeatherSlots
nodes.summary: Builds display slots (location, current condition with icon, min/max temperatures) from fetched Open-Meteo data, falling back to placeholders when data is missing.

nodes.name: fetchOpenMeteoWeather
nodes.summary: Fetches current weather and daily min/max temperatures from the Open-Meteo API for given coordinates and stores the result in module state.

nodes.name: fetchCurrentLocation
nodes.summary: Effective (last-declared) version that loads weather for a fixed location and re-renders the weather pills; two earlier geolocation-based declarations are shadowed by it.
nodes.languageNotes: Function declaration hoisting means this third definition wins over the two earlier fetchCurrentLocation implementations.

nodes.name: createReelItems
nodes.summary: Builds a 3D cylinder reel for one category: computes segment count and radius, creates scene/drum/tile elements with rotateX transforms, and marks the selected front tile.

nodes.name: renderArchive
nodes.summary: Renders the weekly archive (entries since week start) and the full history list, decorating jackpot entries with badges and formatted timestamps.

nodes.name: saveResultToArchive
nodes.summary: Appends a spin result (with timestamp and jackpot flag) to the archive, caps its length, persists to localStorage, and re-renders the archive lists.

nodes.name: renderResult
nodes.summary: Fills the result and summary views with the spun combination's labels, swatch color, weather pills, and jackpot styling, then saves to archive and scrolls the summary into view.

nodes.name: getColorByName
nodes.summary: Maps Korean clothing color names (red, blue, beige, etc.) to hex color values for the result swatch.

nodes.name: ensureAudio
nodes.summary: Lazily creates the shared AudioContext on first use and resumes it if suspended, returning null when Web Audio is unavailable.

nodes.name: playTone
nodes.summary: Plays a single oscillator tone at a given frequency and duration with an exponential gain ramp, used as the building block for spin/result/jackpot sounds.

nodes.name: launchConfetti
nodes.summary: Creates a full-screen canvas and animates falling, rotating confetti particles with requestAnimationFrame, removing the canvas when the show ends.

nodes.name: celebrateJackpot
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the jackpot celebration: confetti launch, jackpot sound, temporary banner element, and a shake animation on the slot machine.

nodes.name: playSpin
nodes.summary: Animates one reel's 3D drum rotation over multiple turns to land on a target index, using requestAnimationFrame with cubic ease-out and toggling spin/jackpot CSS classes.

nodes.name: runSlotMachine
nodes.summary: Main spin flow: rolls the jackpot probability, picks a jackpot combo or random options per reel, staggers the three reel spins, then renders the result and triggers celebration or result sound.

nodes.name: updateStreak
nodes.summary: Increments the daily visit streak when the last visit date differs from today and persists streak and timestamp to localStorage.

nodes.name: loadState
nodes.summary: Loads the persisted garden state from localStorage, validating pixel coordinates and rebuilding history when absent, with a console warning on parse failure.

nodes.name: saveState
nodes.summary: Persists the garden state to localStorage and broadcasts a state-update message to other tabs over BroadcastChannel.

nodes.name: renderPalette
nodes.summary: Builds the 16-color swatch buttons, marks the active color, and re-renders itself when a swatch is selected.

nodes.name: updateStats
nodes.summary: Updates the dashboard: progress count and bar fill, unique gardener count, and cooldown status text based on the active mode.

nodes.name: hexToRgb
nodes.summary: Converts a 3- or 6-digit hex color string into an {r,g,b} object for color-blending math.

nodes.name: getDisplayColor
nodes.summary: Computes a pixel's display color by blending its palette color toward white based on how recently it was planted, so new pixels 'grow' into full saturation.

nodes.name: drawGuideLines
nodes.summary: Draws faint quadratic-curve sketch guides on the garden canvas as a communal drawing outline, toggleable via the guide button.

nodes.name: render
nodes.summary: Main canvas draw pass: clears and paints the background, draws planted or playback pixels with age-based colors, animates recently planted cells with a pop effect, then overlays guides and refreshes the minimap.

nodes.name: renderMinimap
nodes.summary: Draws a scaled-down overview of the garden's pixels onto the minimap canvas, using playback pixels during timelapse.

nodes.name: plantPixel
nodes.summary: Handles a planting click: rejects during timelapse, occupied cells, or active cooldown, then records the pixel with gardener id and timestamp, starts its pop animation, persists, and updates stats.

nodes.name: stopTimelapse
nodes.summary: Halts timelapse playback, clears its timer, resets the button label, and re-renders the live garden state.

nodes.name: startTimelapse
nodes.summary: Replays the planting history chronologically: sorts the timeline, steps through entries on a timer painting each pixel into a playback buffer, and stops when finished.

nodes.name: main.jsx
nodes.summary: React application entry point that mounts the App component into the #root DOM element using React 19's createRoot, wrapped in StrictMode, and loads the global stylesheet.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the modern react-dom/client createRoot API (React 18+) rather than the legacy ReactDOM.render.

nodes.name: vite.config.js
nodes.summary: Vite build configuration for the React starter app, registering the official @vitejs/plugin-react plugin to enable JSX transformation and Fast Refresh.

nodes.name: dog.webp
nodes.summary: Shared WebP image asset of a dog, used as a sample picture by the HTML and CSS course exercises in this learning repository.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Minimal project README containing only the GitHub repository URL (https://github.com/chrusterd/hancom), with no further documentation.
